name: High Privilege Virtual Machine Performing Suspicious Actions
notes: This rule triggers when a virtual machine with a higher privilege (e.g. machine can access storage that contains personal information such as credit card numbers) performs suspicious activities. This action can indicate a virtual machine changing the permissions to perform malicious actions.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization, BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud
when any of Machine Identifier (custom) are contained in any of Resources with High Privilege Roles - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
TD_list: APPLY High Privilege Virtual Machine Performing Suspicious Actions on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Virtualization, BB:DeviceDefinition: Cloud
AND when any of Machine Identifier (custom) are contained in any of Resources with High Privilege Roles - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
RED_list: Event name: High Privilege Virtual Machine Performing Suspicious Actions
Event description: Detects a virtual machine with a higher privilege (e.g. machine can access storage that contains personal information such as credit card numbers) performs suspicious activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
